items.title: Growing Technological Opacity and the Social Brain
items.author: François Osiurak, Giovanni Federico
items.content: <article>Perspectives on Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Thanks to our remarkable ability to transmit technical content, our technologies have become more sophisticated. Intuitively, one might assume that this evolution has imposed greater demands on the technical brain. However, recent neuroscientific research ...</article>

items.title: Bringing the Reading Sciences Into the Classroom: Insights for Phonics Instruction
items.author: Tobias Ungerer, Kathleen Rastle, Blair C. Armstrong
items.content: <article>Perspectives on Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Teaching phonics—that is, systematic mappings between letters and sounds—plays a foundational role in how children learn to read in alphabetical writing systems. Although the reading sciences yield important insights into the factors underlying effective ...</article>
